title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. hispanica
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. hispanica, commonly known as the Iberian columbine or wild columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. hispanica include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
